behavior_summary: This script implements the inspect ('U8P_DEADEW::look') and use behaviors for a dead-body (corpse) item.
U8P_DEADEW::DEADEW::look: when a player/actor examines the corpse it produces a short barked description 'dead body ' and then returns/suspends.

U8P_DEADEW::DEADEW::use: when a player/actor uses/interacts with the corpse it reads the item's status word and tests bit 0x0100. If that bit is clear it closes the item's gump/UI (Item::closeGump) and returns. If the bit is set it makes a random roll (urandom(100)); on a rare roll (> 95) the corpse barks 'A curse upon you, thief!' and the script suspends, otherwise it opens the corpse's gump/UI with parameter 0x0C (Item::openGump).
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEADEW::DEADEW::look: linear behavior — construct/prepare string 'dead body ', call Item::bark(target, string), then suspend/return.

U8P_DEADEW::DEADEW::use: conditional branch on Item::getStatus(item) & 0x0100. If the mask is clear -> Item::closeGump(item) -> return. If set -> generate urandom(100) and compare: roll > 95 -> Item::bark(item, 'A curse upon you, thief!') -> suspend; else -> Item::openGump(item, 0x0C).
open_questions: What is the semantic meaning of the status bit 0x0100 (appears to gate open vs close behavior, but exact semantic name/meaning is unknown).
Does the barked string 'dead body ' get combined with additional dynamic text (e.g., owner name) elsewhere, or is it the full displayed inspect text?
Which process id (pid) ordering is passed into Item::bark in each call (caller vs target) — parameter roles are inferred from calling convention but not explicitly named in the disassembly.
Exact gameplay effect and duration of the 'suspend' after the curse bark (whether it prevents subsequent actions or simply halts the script until next event) is inferred but not explicit.

main_function.name: U8P_DEADEW::look
main_function.purpose: Produce the short inspect/vocalization text when a corpse item is examined ('U8P_DEADEW::look' event).
entry_conditions: Event 0: actor/player examines or 'looks at' the corpse item (inspect trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_DEADEW::look
function_map.role: Emit the corpse's inspect description via Item::bark and then suspend/return.

function_map.name: U8P_DEADEW::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' interactions: decide to close or open the corpse's gump, with a small random chance to emit an anti-theft curse instead of opening.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine call to show/emit short text associated with an item/process to a player (used for inspect text and curse message).

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Engine call returning the item's status/flags bitfield (used to test the 0x0100 bit).

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Engine call to close the item's UI/gump.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Generate a pseudorandom integer in range 0..(n-1); used for chance to trigger the curse bark.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Open the item's UI/gump with a specified parameter (here 0x0C).

external_calls.purpose: Display the corpse's inspect description string 'dead body ' to the examining process/player.
Read the item's status bitfield to test whether bit 0x0100 is set (decides close vs open behavior).
Close the corpse's gump/UI when the 0x0100 status bit is clear.
Produce a random integer (0..99) to determine a rare chance (roll > 95) to trigger the curse message instead of opening the gump.
Emit the curse text 'A curse upon you, thief!' on a rare random roll; this branch also suspends further script action.
Open the corpse's gump/UI with parameter 0x0C when status bit 0x0100 is set and the random roll does not trigger the curse branch.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the inspect ('U8P_DEADEW::look') and use behaviors for a dead-body (corpse) item.
